list.name: Changes
list.comment: 18th Commit 07/05/2026: Updated evaluation of filters, concluded that more complex algebraic structures needed to be prompted to be discovered or an active search for theories on Pomerance Proof is needed

task.summary: Default task
Initial commit
Second commit 04/30/2026
Third commit 05/14/2026
Third commit 05/14/2026
Fourth commit 05/14/2026: Added 20k 7-11 digits
Fifth commit 05/16/2026: Added 10k 12-15 digits
Sixth commit 05/16/2026: Reordered directory, put non-final testing data into /process_time_testing_data
Seventh commit 05/16/2026: Fixed naming issue

Eighth commit 05/25/2026: 1. Sorted data, removed duplicates 2. Fixed algorithmic problems of failing primes caused by ignoring supersingular elliptic curves when searching for triples 3. Performed hypothesis testing on number of trials for primes with the same digit length, conclusion: REJECT H0 that all numbers are generated from the same parameter. This indicates that the difficulty of finding triple for primes similar in size differs significantly and each prime might have unique properties to discover.

Ninth commit 06/01/2026: 1. Refined the trials counting algorithm to only include trials finding A 2. Refined the trials recording algorithm for better accuracy (the previous lc*threads tends to overestimate trials count) 3. Included two parameters (a) (b) in difficulty_parameters.py for evaluating difficulty 4. Initial test for Order-Restricted LRT on primes within a small range (regional largest=1.1*smallest) for H0: p1>=p2>=...>=pk vs H1: unconstrained, rejection rate ranges from 10%-30%
Tenth Commit 06/01/2026: Fixed problems for failure in uploading lrt_data
Eleventh Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
12th Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant (in reports/regression)

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant (in reports/regression)

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant (in reports/regression)

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant (in reports/regression)

15th Commit 06/21/2026: 1. Refined isotonic LRT, changed into H0: global #trials increasing, all results show REJECT H0 with p-value ~0, which indicates instead of strict monotone increasing the difficulty has significant fluctuations. 2. Refined regression, changed parameter a into categorical data and tested regression under different magnitude range (3-13, 10-13, 12-13) which shows a tendency of convergence to 1 of the coefficient of log(sqrt(p)), with coefficient of log(b) shows a tendency of convergence to 0 and a converging to 3&4 as magnitude increases. One explanation is that log(sqrt(p)) and log(b) has dependent relations (with b having sqrt((p+1-m)^2-4*p) as average) and that the model has the tendency to keep only the parameter log(sqrt(p)) and absorb log(b) into it, since the impact of the absolute magnitude dominates the impact of b. One evidence to this explanation is that while the coefficient of p converging to 1 and the coefficient of b converging to 0, the difference of these two coefficients keeps close to 1. As the magnitude continues to increase it is hypothesized that the two parameters will converge into a single parameter log(sqrt(p)).

16th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). The Current difficulty is that the agent tends to fail creating an effective strategy besides guessing random patterns that are ineffective for optimization.

16th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). The Current difficulty is that the agent tends to fail creating an effective strategy besides guessing random patterns that are ineffective for optimization.

16th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). The Current difficulty is that the agent tends to fail creating an effective strategy besides guessing random patterns that are ineffective for optimization.

17th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). An effective progress is that the agent is currently able to discover heuristic & statistical patterns in the data and shrink the searching space despite ignoring some potentially correct answers, but the negative impact will be minimal for large primes. The next steps: 1. Optimize training & prompting strategy to encourage discovery of more advanced algebraic geometry patterns, 2. If needed, decrease false negative rate

18th Commit 07/05/2026: Updated evaluation of filters, concluded that more complex algebraic structures needed to be prompted to be discovered or an active search for theories on Pomerance Proof is needed

MESSAGE.value: Sixth commit 05/16/2026: Reordered directory, put non-final testing data into /process_time_testing_data

Eighth commit 05/25/2026: 1. Sorted data, removed duplicates 2. Fixed algorithmic problems of failing primes caused by ignoring supersingular elliptic curves when searching for triples 3. Performed hypothesis testing on number of trials for primes with the same digit length, conclusion: REJECT H0 that all numbers are generated from the same parameter. This indicates that the difficulty of finding triple for primes similar in size differs significantly and each prime might have unique properties to discover.

Ninth commit 06/01/2026: 1. Refined the trials counting algorithm to only include trials finding A 2. Refined the trials recording algorithm for better accuracy (the previous lc*threads tends to overestimate trials count) 3. Included two parameters (a) (b) in difficulty_parameters.py for evaluating difficulty 4. Initial test for Order-Restricted LRT on primes within a small range (regional largest=1.1*smallest) for H0: p1>=p2>=...>=pk vs H1: unconstrained, rejection rate ranges from 10%-30%
Tenth Commit 06/01/2026: Fixed problems for failure in uploading lrt_data
Eleventh Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
12th Commit 06/01/2026: 1. Added 10-12 digits tests 2. Compared p-values under 10 trials & 5 trials
13th Commit 06/07/2026: Corrected the calculation of difficulty parameter b: class number

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant.

14th Commit 06/14/2026: 1. Compared theoretical success probability with experimental probability estimated by trials (in reports/p_stats/convergence_report, with sample size n=100 gives a 9.58% error) 2. Updated isotonic test, results rejection rate ranges ~10%-24% (data in data/lrt_data, reports in reports/lrt_reports and p-value analysis in txt file "PValue_Global_Analysis_*digits" in each digit folder) 3. Performed linear/log-linear regression on trials and sqrt(p), difficulty parameter a and b, results show that correlation is significant (in reports/regression)

15th Commit 06/21/2026: 1. Refined isotonic LRT, changed into H0: global #trials increasing, all results show REJECT H0 with p-value ~0, which indicates instead of strict monotone increasing the difficulty has significant fluctuations. 2. Refined regression, changed parameter a into categorical data and tested regression under different magnitude range (3-13, 10-13, 12-13) which shows a tendency of convergence to 1 of the coefficient of log(sqrt(p)), with coefficient of log(b) shows a tendency of convergence to 0 and a converging to 3&4 as magnitude increases. One explanation is that log(sqrt(p)) and log(b) has dependent relations (with b having sqrt((p+1-m)^2-4*p) as average) and that the model has the tendency to keep only the parameter log(sqrt(p)) and absorb log(b) into it, since the impact of the absolute magnitude dominates the impact of b. One evidence to this explanation is that while the coefficient of p converging to 1 and the coefficient of b converging to 0, the difference of these two coefficients keeps close to 1. As the magnitude continues to increase it is hypothesized that the two parameters will converge into a single parameter log(sqrt(p)).

16th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). The Current difficulty is that the agent tends to fail creating an effective strategy besides guessing random patterns that are ineffective for optimization.

17th Commit 06/30/2026: Initial trial for agent-assisted algorithm optimization. Methodology: Prompted AI to discover both deterministic and statistic patterns in the training data and encapsulate its hypothesized filter mechanism in a function, aiming to create a library of filter strategies (in filter_strategies.py). An effective progress is that the agent is currently able to discover heuristic & statistical patterns in the data and shrink the searching space despite ignoring some potentially correct answers, but the negative impact will be minimal for large primes. The next steps: 1. Optimize training & prompting strategy to encourage discovery of more advanced algebraic geometry patterns, 2. If needed, decrease false negative rate

18th Commit 07/05/2026: Updated evaluation of filters, concluded that more complex algebraic structures needed to be prompted to be discovered or an active search for theories on Pomerance Proof is needed

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: 18th Commit 07/05/2026: Updated evaluation of filters, concluded that more complex algebraic structures needed to be prompted to be discovered or an active search for theories on Pomerance Proof is needed
